title: Prove delegation expiry narrowing is sound

artifacts.description: Certora-style property spec with 4 invariants: (1) delegateeExpiry <= delegatorExpiry for all valid witnesses, (2) delegateeExpiry > currentTimestamp is required for proof acceptance, (3) comparisons operate on range-checked 64-bit values not raw field elements, (4) no field-element wraparound can satisfy LessThan comparator with out-of-range inputs
Circom circuit fragment enforcing range-checked 64-bit expiry comparison using Num2Bits(64) decomposition before LessThan, ensuring wraparound is impossible

Test harness generating boundary witnesses: expiry=2^64-1, expiry=0, expiry=delegatorExpiry+1 (should reject), expiry=delegatorExpiry-1 (should accept), expiry=delegatorExpiry (should accept), currentTimestamp edge cases
Write-up of soundness argument, identified attack vectors (field element wraparound), constraint count justification, and interpretation of spec invariant results

steps: Audit existing Delegation circuit source for the delegateeExpiry <= delegatorExpiry constraint; locate the LessThan component instantiation and verify it receives Num2Bits-decomposed 64-bit signals, not raw field element inputs
Identify the missing or truncated constraint: confirm whether delegateeExpiry <= currentTimestamp rejection is explicitly enforced or implicitly assumed; document the gap
Draft invariant I1 in spec: for all witnesses w where proof verifies, w.delegateeExpiry <= w.delegatorExpiry holds in Z (not F_p arithmetic)
Draft invariant I2 in spec: for all witnesses w where proof verifies, w.delegateeExpiry > w.currentTimestamp (proof rejects expired delegatee credentials)
Draft invariant I3 in spec: the LessThan comparator inputs are range-checked to [0, 2^64) via Num2Bits(64) before comparison, ruling out field-element wraparound where p - 1 < 2^64 mod p could pass
Draft invariant I4 in spec: no witness with delegateeExpiry >= 2^64 can satisfy the range-check sub-circuit, making the comparator input domain provably bounded
Implement the DelegationExpiryCheck circuit fragment with explicit Num2Bits(64) for both expiry signals before feeding into LessThan(64), add GreaterThan(64) for currentTimestamp check

Write boundary witness generator in the test harness: cases expiry=0, expiry=1, expiry=delegatorExpiry-1 (valid), expiry=delegatorExpiry (valid boundary), expiry=delegatorExpiry+1 (must reject), expiry=2^64-1 (range boundary), expiry=2^64 (must fail range check)
Run snarkjs witness generation for each boundary case; assert expected accept/reject outcomes match; capture constraint satisfaction status
Count active constraints in DelegationExpiryCheck circuit (estimate: 64 bits * 2 signals for Num2Bits + LessThan internals + GreaterThan internals)
Write formal analysis doc summarizing the soundness argument, the wraparound attack vector, and how Num2Bits decomposition closes it
dependencies: snarkjs (witness generation and proof verification for boundary tests)
existing Delegation.circom (parent circuit this fragment integrates into)
